rulings.Case Title: PATRINA HARRISON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Patrina Harrison Iso Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 22, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. This matter is continued on the court's own motion to June 5, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 302. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARSHALL HOLLAND VS. THE MOSSER COMPANIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer Defendants Deposition Questions And Request For Monetary Sanctions **Redacted**
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 22, 2025 Line 8. DEFENDANT MOSSER COMPANIES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer Defendants Deposition Questions And Request For Monetary Sanctions **Redacted** Defendant The Mosser Companies, Inc's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer Defendants Deposition Questions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Defendant brings its motion under Code of Civil procedure section 2025.480. Subdivision (b) of that section provides, "This motion shall be made no later than 60 days after the completion of the record of the deposition, and shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040." "A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion," according to Section 2016.040, "shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion." The court finds Defendant has failed to Plaintiff failed to demonstrate she made a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by this motion. Defense counsel's "attemp[t] [on the record at the April 2, 2025 deposition] to meet and confer with Plaintiff and his counsel regarding Plaintiff's refusal to produce information and documents relevant to this case" does not evidence a good faith a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of the issues regarding the deposition testimony. (See Stonhaus Decl. para. 10.) Nor does defense counsel's response to Plaintiff's counsel's April 8, 2025, late afternoon email demonstrate a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution. At that point, Defendant's papers were written and, indeed, Defendant filed its papers quite soon after that. The record does not show Defendant acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Defendant and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $500 as sanctions, payable to Plaintiff, payment no later than by April 30, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 6, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER CASHION VS. WESTFIELD, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 22, 2025 Line 9. 1 - DEFENDANTs TOWNSHIP BUILDING SERVICES, INC., WESTFIELD SF CENTER LP, WESTFIELD PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, and S.F. CENTRE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP NAMED AS WESTFIELD MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants Township Building Services, Inc., Westfield SF Center LP, Westfield Property Management and SF Center Limited Partnership's motion to strike is DENIED. "Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof," (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (Code of Civil Procedure section 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (Code of Civil Procedure section 452.) Here, Defendants' motion fails because it is based on a condition precedent that has not come to pass. Defendants contend Plaintiff's punitive damages allegations and prayer should be stricken because they are premised on Plaintiff's tort claims-the first and second causes of action, which cannot survive demurrer. Those claims, however, survived demurrer. Thus the motion to strike must be denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
